description:
You are acting as a junior penetration-tester who has just received the raw output of a TCP port scan that somebody else ran earlier.  
    The scan result is already stored on-disk at  

        /home/user/vuln_scans/initial_scan.txt  

    Your job is to distil that raw scan into a tiny, machine-readable log that only lists the target IP, the number of open ports, and then each open port with its associated service.

    1. Locate the file exactly at the path given above (there is **no sub-directory level difference**).  
    2. From that file, identify every line that is an **open** TCP port.  
       • Lines that represent an open port contain the string “open” as their second column.  
    3. Create a new file named  

        /home/user/vuln_scans/open_ports_summary.log  

       The file **must overwrite** any previous file of the same name.  
    4. Write the summary to that file in the **five-row exact format** shown below.  
       • Use plain ASCII text, no colour codes, tabs or extra spaces.  
       • Every field is separated by exactly one single space.  
       • No blank lines are allowed anywhere in the file.  
       • The port/service pairs must keep the same ordering as they appear in the original scan file.

       Exact required format (5+ rows):

           Target: <IP_ADDRESS>
           Total_Open_Ports: <NUMBER>
           PORT SERVICE
           <port_1/tcp> <service_1>
           <port_2/tcp> <service_2>
           ...
    
       Example of what the finished file should look like once your commands have run (the concrete values will, of course, depend on the data actually found in initial_scan.txt):

           Target: 10.0.0.5
           Total_Open_Ports: 3
           PORT SERVICE
           22/tcp ssh
           80/tcp http
           443/tcp https

    5. After creating the file, display its contents to the terminal so that a supervising human can visually confirm the result.

    A correct solution therefore consists of no more than 3-4 shell commands (directory creation is **not** needed because /home/user/vuln_scans already exists) that:
       • Parse the supplied scan file,
       • Count the open ports,
       • Produce /home/user/vuln_scans/open_ports_summary.log in the exact structure shown above,
       • And finally output that file to STDOUT.

    The automated grader will check:
       • That /home/user/vuln_scans/open_ports_summary.log exists,
       • That it contains exactly 5 or more non-empty lines,
       • That the first line starts with “Target:”,
       • That the second line starts with “Total_Open_Ports:” with a correct numerical count,
       • That the third line is exactly “PORT SERVICE” (without quotes),
       • That every subsequent line matches the “<number>/tcp <service>” pattern,
       • And that the ordering of those subsequent lines is the same as they appear in initial_scan.txt.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file that the agent must read -->
    File path: /home/user/vuln_scans/initial_scan.txt
    Exact contents (5 lines, ending with a trailing newline):

        Host: 10.0.0.5 (target-machine)  Status: Up
        PORT     STATE SERVICE
        21/tcp   open  ftp
        22/tcp   open  ssh
        80/tcp   open  http
        111/tcp  closed rpcbind
        5432/tcp open  postgresql

    Expected artefact to be produced by the agent:
    File path: /home/user/vuln_scans/open_ports_summary.log
    Expected exact contents (no leading spaces, each line terminated by '\n'):

        Target: 10.0.0.5
        Total_Open_Ports: 4
        PORT SERVICE
        21/tcp ftp
        22/tcp ssh
        80/tcp http
        5432/tcp postgresql
name: task_000000_cf2a15e4
